Title: OeBAS and CYP716C67 catalyze the biosynthesis of health-beneficial triterpenoids in olive (Olea europaea) fruits

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the major scaffold precursor of the target products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing step characterized in olive fruit triterpenoid biosynthesis; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Olea europaea; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in pYES2-DEST52 for yeast; pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression in N. benthamiana; native expression analyzed in olive
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in fruits; low in other organs relative to fruit
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in olive fruits; functional products detected in yeast and N. benthamiana leaves upon heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive compounds with applications in agri-food, cosmetics, and pharmaceutical industries
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages pooled for expression analysis; transient agro-infiltration in N. benthamiana; galactose induction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; RNA-seq coexpression/expression profiling; RT-qPCR; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae followed by GC-MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with pathway reconstruction and GC-MS; marker-trait association analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of β-amyrin-derived olive fruit triterpenoids by supplying the β-amyrin scaffold required for erythrodiol, oleanolic acid, and maslinic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeBAS was identified from olive OSC candidates and shown to be highly expressed in fruit. 2. Yeast expression demonstrated that OeBAS specifically produces β-amyrin, unlike OeMAS which makes both α- and β-amyrin. 3. Co-expression with CYP716A48 and CYP716C67 in N. benthamiana reconstituted the pathway to oleanolic and maslinic acids. 4. A nearby marker on chromosome 15 was significantly associated with maslinic acid content in olive fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oxidosqualene cyclase (OSC); squalene cyclase family; InterPro IPR018333
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics/RNA-seq expression datasets from olive organs (Ramírez-Tejero et al., 2020); genomic mining from olive genome/transcriptome datasets; ddRAD sequencing-based marker association data from Mariotti et al. 2020
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two nearly identical alleles (v1 and v2) were cloned and both produced the same product; v1 was used for further experiments. OeBAS is located on chromosome 15 in cv Leccino and chromosome 15 in cv Arbequina.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Triterpenoid C-2α oxidase / oleanolic acid and ursolic acid 2α-hydroxylase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Downstream oxidation of oleanane- and ursane-type triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-2α oxidation/hydroxylation of oleanolic acid and ursolic acid to form maslinic acid and corosolic acid, respectively
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing downstream oxidation step characterized in olive; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression in N. benthamiana; native expression analyzed in olive
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in fruits; co-expressed with OeBAS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in olive fruits; active in N. benthamiana when co-expressed with upstream pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Health-beneficial bioactive compounds with applications in agri-food, cosmetics, and pharmaceutical industries
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agro-infiltration in N. benthamiana; fruit organ expression analysis in olive
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; RNA-seq coexpression analysis; RT-qPCR; combinatorial transient expression in N. benthamiana with OeBAS/OeMAS and CYP716A48 followed by GC-MS; comparison with C. asiatica CYP716C11 and M. truncatula CYP72A67 products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of maslinic acid and corosolic acid by catalyzing C-2α oxidation of oleanolic and ursolic acids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716C67 was identified from fruit-expressed CYP716 candidates co-expressed with OeBAS. 2. It catalyzes C-2α oxidation of oleanolic and ursolic acids to produce maslinic and corosolic acids in reconstructed pathways. 3. It does not accept α- or β-amyrin directly as substrate, requiring prior C-28 oxidation products. 4. Markers on chromosome 13 near CYP716C67 were significantly associated with oleanolic acid content in olive fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics/RNA-seq expression datasets from olive organs; genomic mining of olive CYP450 repertoire; ddRAD sequencing-based marker association data
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two allelic forms (v1 and v2) showed identical activity. The enzyme is unable to oxidize α- or β-amyrin directly and acts after CYP716A48-mediated C-28 oxidation. Located on chromosome 13 in cv Leccino and Arbequina.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C-28 oxidation of oleanane- and ursane-type triterpene scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream oxidation step downstream of scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oleanolic acid; ursolic acid; erythrodiol; uvaol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential C-28 oxidation of β-amyrin and α-amyrin through alcohol and aldehyde intermediates to generate oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, with erythrodiol and uvaol as intermediates/minor products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors required for CYP716C67-mediated synthesis of target products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Previously characterized pathway step; important but not the primary novel focus of this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 transient expression in N. benthamiana; native expression analyzed in olive
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in fruits and other organs; pattern overall comparable to RNA-seq and consistent with a role in triterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in N. benthamiana when co-expressed with OeBAS or OeMAS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Oleanolic acid; ursolic acid; maslinic acid; corosolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive health-promoting triterpenoids in olive fruits and oil
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agro-infiltration in N. benthamiana; fruit/organ expression analysis in olive
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana with GC-MS; RT-qPCR; comparison with prior functional characterization cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies oleanolic and ursolic acid intermediates necessary for downstream formation of maslinic and corosolic acids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A48v1 from cv Leccino was included to reconstruct the olive triterpenoid pathway. 2. Co-expression with OeBAS generated oleanolic acid and erythrodiol, while co-expression with OeMAS generated oleanolic acid, ursolic acid, erythrodiol, and uvaol. 3. Its products serve as substrates for CYP716C67 to form maslinic and corosolic acids.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics/RNA-seq expression datasets from olive organs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although essential to complete pathway reconstruction, CYP716A48 was previously characterized and is not the main newly discovered gene in this study.
